name: Hugo Casillas — 7-day NEPQ cadence
description: Hugo's dedicated 7-day plan instantiated from the seven_day_nepq_template. Plan-day cadence: Day 1 email (Highland Orchard anchor + walkthrough video offer), Days 2/3/5/7 passive, Day 4 SMS conditional on no reply, Day 6 email conditional on no reply, plus a Day-5 quote-expiry SMS one-shot for the May 1 14-day window. R1 comms heartbeat watches Close every 10 min weekdays 9-19 ET. Tasting-prep brief lands May 16 6pm if Hugo confirms the May 17 tasting. Andre is in the loop on every send via Slack DM. Cadence pauses the moment Hugo replies — R1 hands off to Andre and R2 holds.

batch_policy.notes: Per-lead cadence — one Hugo. Batching is the wrong frame here.

principles: Stop selling to Hugo. Start selling with Hugo. He reads templates the way Andre reads cereal-box backs.
Highland Orchard is the trust anchor — used once, used right, in the first email.
Bring the fiancée in by Day 6 — she's the dietary anchor and likely the deciding voice.
Andre sees every send before it lands as a Slack DM receipt. R2 never goes silent.

nodes.label: R2 plan executor — 10:30am weekdays
nodes.notes: The plan executor — fires every weekday morning, decides whether today's plan-day move ships.
nodes.description: Each weekday at 10:30am ET, the plan executor wakes, reads plan-start-date from Drive, and asks: what's today's move?

nodes.label: R1 comms heartbeat — every 10 min
nodes.notes: Watches Close for any inbound from Hugo. Pings Andre on Slack the moment something lands.
nodes.description: Every 10 minutes during business hours, R1 polls Close for new inbound and pages Andre if Hugo speaks.

nodes.label: Close: lead.activity (Hugo)
nodes.notes: Optional faster path — Close webhook fires when Hugo replies, beats the 10-min poll.
nodes.description: If a Close webhook is wired, Hugo's reply lands here within seconds and pre-empts R1's next poll.

nodes.label: Run on demand
nodes.notes: Manual override — Andre re-runs today's move from the lead box.
nodes.description: A human button on Hugo's lead box that re-fires today's planned move.

nodes.label: R3 quote expiry — May 1 9am
nodes.notes: One-shot — fires the quote-expiry SMS on May 1 9am, the 14-day window close.
nodes.description: May 1 at 9am, the quote 14-day window closes. R3 fires a single SMS reminder to Hugo.

nodes.label: R4 tasting prep — May 16 6pm
nodes.notes: Only fires if Hugo has confirmed the May 17 2pm tasting in Close.
nodes.description: Saturday May 16 at 6pm, R4 drops the pre-tasting brief into Andre's Slack — only if Hugo confirmed.

nodes.label: people/hugo_casillas.json
nodes.notes: Hugo's identity record — voice, dietary, fiancée context, Highland Orchard anchor.
nodes.description: The bedrock identity record Hugo's agent reads to keep messages personal and on-target.

nodes.label: people/andre_raw.json
nodes.notes: Andre's voice profile — lowercase, peer, no template smell.
nodes.description: Andre's voice handling notes. Every outbound mirrors his register.

nodes.label: Drive: plan-start-date + last-fired
nodes.notes: Three small Drive files hold the cadence's working memory. Escalation-pause file gates R2.
nodes.description: The cadence's persistent state lives in three Drive files. The escalation-pause flag is the safety hatch.

nodes.label: Compute plan day
nodes.notes: Day 1 = plan-start day. Day > 7 → DM Andre 'plan window complete' and exit.
nodes.description: Subtracts plan-start from today. If we're past Day 7, the plan window is closed.

nodes.label: Escalation pause?
nodes.notes: Hard safety gate. If the file exists with any content, R2 holds and DMs Andre.
nodes.description: The hard pause. R2 will not send if R1 (or Andre) has flagged an escalation.

nodes.label: Today's move already fired?
nodes.notes: Idempotency — if today's slot is in last-move-fired, exit silently.
nodes.description: Idempotency check — never double-fire a slot in a single day.

nodes.label: Inbound from Hugo since last move?
nodes.notes: Day 4 + Day 6 are gated on no-reply. Day 1 always fires.
nodes.description: Counts Close inbound activity since the last fire. Used by Days 4 and 6 to skip-on-reply.

nodes.label: Day 1 — Email (Highland anchor)

config.subject: Following up — and a different approach for your tasting

nodes.notes: Day 1 always fires on first Day-1 run. Highland Orchard reference + walkthrough video offer + weekday call ask.
nodes.description: Day 1 — the email that resets the relationship. Highland Orchard anchor, fiancée invitation, walkthrough offer.

nodes.label: Day 4 — SMS (conditional)
nodes.notes: Lowercase, 30 words, Wed/Thu evening ask. Skips if Hugo replied since Day 1.
nodes.description: Day 4 — the casual nudge. Lowercase, specific window, no template smell. Skips on reply.

nodes.label: Day 6 — Email (conditional)

config.subject: One more thing before the competing quotes come in

nodes.notes: The 'I won't keep nudging' email. Empowers fiancée to engage independently. Skips on reply.
nodes.description: Day 6 — the consultative memo. Educates Hugo on what to look for in competing quotes; opens a direct line to his fiancée.

nodes.label: Quote-expiry SMS — May 1
nodes.notes: One-shot — 'quote 14-day window closes today. extend, or ready to talk?'
nodes.description: May 1 quote-expiry one-shot. Real deadline, real message. Hugo respects it.

nodes.label: Rodbot voice mirror
nodes.notes: Drafts each outbound in Andre's voice from the templates. No lowercase-uppercase mistakes, no 'circling back.'
nodes.description: Before any send, Rodbot reads Andre's voice profile + Hugo's context and renders the draft.

nodes.label: R4 — pre-tasting brief generator
nodes.notes: Composes the May 16 6pm Slack brief: everything Andre needs walking into the May 17 2pm tasting.
nodes.description: R4's brief composer. Pulls Hugo's record + Highland status and writes the night-before walking-in brief.

nodes.label: Classify Hugo's reply
nodes.notes: R1's classifier. Routine = green ping. Anything borderline = pause + page Andre.
nodes.description: Rodbot reads any inbound and decides: green ping or red pause. Borderline writes the escalation-pause flag.

nodes.label: Escalation?
nodes.notes: Pricing pushback, named competitor, fiancée engaging directly = escalation.
nodes.description: Hard signals that flip the cadence to manual: a number from a competitor, Hugo's fiancée writing, hostile tone.

nodes.label: Slack DM — Andre
nodes.notes: Two formats: 🟢 routine reply ping (verbatim text + plan's next move), 🚨 escalation alert (system holds).
nodes.description: The single Slack DM Andre sees. Either a green inbound ping with what plan says next, or a red 'cadence held' page.

nodes.label: Slack receipt — every send
nodes.notes: After every successful Close send, Andre gets the verbatim copy.
nodes.description: Every send mirrors to Slack with the verbatim body so Andre never wonders what went out.

nodes.label: _ledger/activity.jsonl
nodes.notes: Append-only audit. Every R2 fire + R1 ping writes one line.
nodes.description: The append-only audit trail. No mutation by this workflow leaves without a ledger entry.

nodes.label: state/today.html
nodes.notes: Andre's morning briefing surface — Hugo's row updates with current plan day + last move + watch-fors.
nodes.description: Hugo's row on Andre's daily briefing page. Updated by every R2 fire so the briefing is fresh by 8am.

nodes.label: Mission-control: Hugo cell
nodes.notes: Live tile on the grid showing Hugo's cadence health.
nodes.description: A single mission-control tile: where Hugo is in the 7-day arc, what fires next, whether the cadence is held.

connections.label: read state
connections.description: R2 reads plan-start, last-fired, and the escalation-pause flag from Drive on every fire.

connections.label: plan_day
connections.description: Plan-start + today produce the plan_day index.

connections.label: → pause check
connections.description: Once we know the day, we check the pause flag before doing anything.

connections.label: no pause
connections.description: Pause clear → continue. Pause set → DM Andre and exit.

connections.label: fresh slot
connections.description: Slot fresh → check for inbound. Already fired → exit silent.

connections.label: draft compose
connections.description: If the gating clears for today's day, hand the template to Rodbot to render in Andre's voice.

connections.label: Day 1
connections.description: Day 1 — render and send the email.

connections.label: Day 4
connections.description: Day 4 SMS — only if Hugo hasn't replied since Day 1.

connections.label: Day 6
connections.description: Day 6 email — the consultative memo. Skips on reply.

connections.label: voice
connections.description: Rodbot reads Andre's voice profile to mirror lowercase / no-template / peer register.

connections.label: context
connections.description: Rodbot reads Hugo's record so drafts reference Highland Orchard / fiancée / dietary specifically.

connections.label: send receipt
connections.description: Day 1 send → Slack DM to Andre with verbatim body.

connections.label: send receipt
connections.description: Day 4 send → Slack DM receipt.

connections.label: send receipt
connections.description: Day 6 send → Slack DM receipt.

connections.label: poll close
connections.description: R1 polls Close for inbound and feeds new activity into the classifier.

connections.label: fast path
connections.description: Close webhook bypasses the 10-min poll when wired.

connections.label: borderline?
connections.description: Classifier output → escalation gate.

connections.label: write pause flag
connections.description: Borderline → write escalation-paused.txt to Drive. R2 will hold on next fire.

connections.label: page andre
connections.description: Either way (routine or escalation), Andre gets a Slack DM with the verbatim message + plan's next move.

connections.label: May 1 9am
connections.description: R3 fires the one-shot quote-expiry SMS on May 1 at 9am.

connections.label: send receipt
connections.description: Quote-expiry SMS → Slack DM receipt.

connections.label: May 16 6pm
connections.description: R4 wakes only if Hugo's confirmed the May 17 tasting in Close.

connections.label: brief Andre
connections.description: R4 pushes the full tasting-night brief to Andre via Slack DM.

connections.label: on demand
connections.description: Manual run-button bypasses the cron and re-fires today's move.

connections.label: append
connections.description: Every send-receipt appends a line to activity.jsonl.

connections.label: append
connections.description: R1 pings + escalations append to the ledger too.

connections.label: today.html
connections.description: Hugo's row on Andre's morning briefing updates with the latest fire.

connections.label: tile
connections.description: Mission-control tile reflects current plan_day + next-fire ETA.
